proposals.statement: Before agreeing terms, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) itself sued WBD's board: on January 12, 2026 PSKY filed a complaint in the Delaware Court of Chancery against WBD's board of directors (and its Chair Emeritus) during the contested sale process.
proposals.quote: On January 12, 2026, PSKY filed a complaint in the Delaware Court of Chancery against our board of directors (and our Chair Emeritus, Dr.
proposals.locator: Item 8, Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements, Note 20 (Commitments and Contingencies) — Litigation

proposals.statement: PSKY's Delaware Chancery action did not gain traction on an expedited basis: on January 15, 2026 the court denied PSKY's request for expedition, finding PSKY had not demonstrated it would suffer irreparable harm as a WBD stockholder if the litigation were not expedited.
proposals.quote: On January 15, 2026, the Delaware Court of Chancery denied PSKY’s request for expedition, stating that PSKY failed to demonstrate that it would suffer any irreparable harm in its capacity as a stockholder of the Company if the litigation was not expedited, among other reasons.
proposals.locator: Item 8, Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements, Note 20 (Commitments and Contingencies) — Litigation

proposals.statement: A stockholder suit tied to the contested tender-offer process asserts breach of fiduciary duty against WBD's directors, alleging the board failed to disclose material information in the Schedule 14D-9 (filed December 17, 2025) and its January 7, 2026 amendment.
proposals.quote: The suit asserts a claim for breach of fiduciary duty against the directors, alleging that our board of directors failed to disclose material information in both the Solicitation/Recommendation Statement on Schedule 14D-9, filed on December 17, 2025, and the amendment to that Schedule 14D-9, filed on January 7, 2026.
proposals.locator: Item 8, Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements, Note 20 (Commitments and Contingencies) — Litigation

proposals.statement: A merger-objection / disclosure suit was filed against the PSKY deal: on April 2, 2026, the complaint captioned Donna Nicosia v. Samuel Di Piazza, Jr. et al. (N.Y. Sup. Ct.) named WBD, its board, and PSKY as defendants, alleging negligence and negligent misrepresentation for disseminating an allegedly false and misleading proxy statement.

source.title: Warner Bros. Discovery DEFA14A (supplemental proxy disclosure)

proposals.quote: The Complaint names as defendants WBD, the members of the WBD board of directors, and PSKY, and alleges, among other things, claims for negligence and negligent misrepresentation for disseminating an allegedly false and misleading proxy statement.
proposals.locator: Explanatory Note (re Nicosia v. Di Piazza, Index No. 150851/2026), filed in connection with the April 23, 2026 special meeting

proposals.statement: Beyond the single filed suit, WBD also received a wave of stockholder demand letters over the merger proxy: as of the DEFA14A filing, WBD had received 15 demand letters from purported stockholders alleging the proxy statement omitted material information or had disclosure deficiencies in violation of federal securities laws, each demanding corrective disclosure.

source.title: Warner Bros. Discovery DEFA14A (supplemental proxy disclosure)

proposals.quote: In addition, as of the date of this filing, WBD has received 15 demand letters from purported stockholders of WBD in connection with the Merger (the “Demand Letters”). The Demand Letters generally allege that the Proxy Statement omitted purportedly material information that rendered it false and misleading or otherwise had disclosure deficiencies in violation of federal securities laws.
proposals.locator: Explanatory Note, filed in connection with the April 23, 2026 special meeting

proposals.statement: WBD also carries standing pre-merger litigation: a securities class action (Collura) was filed November 25, 2024 in the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York.
proposals.quote: On November 25, 2024, a securities class action complaint was filed in the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York ( Collura v.
proposals.locator: Item 8, Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements, Note 20 (Commitments and Contingencies) — Litigation: Securities Class Action

proposals.statement: A related Consolidated Derivative Action asserts claims on WBD's behalf for breach of fiduciary duty on the same facts as the Collura securities case, and on February 19, 2025 the court stayed it pending a final decision on the motions to dismiss in Collura.
proposals.quote: On February 19, 2025, the Court stayed the Consolidated Derivative Action pending resolution of a final decision on all motions to dismiss the operative complaint in the Collura securities action.
proposals.locator: Item 8, Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements, Note 20 (Commitments and Contingencies) — Litigation: Derivative Litigation

proposals.statement: WBD's 10-K flags additional deal-related litigation as a forward risk: it states litigation may be filed against the board in connection with the PSKY Merger, including putative stockholder complaints or stockholder class action complaints.
proposals.quote: Further, litigation may be filed against the board of directors in connection with the PSKY Merger, including putative stockholder complaints or stockholder class action complaints.
proposals.locator: Item 1A, Risk Factors (risks relating to the PSKY Merger)

proposals.statement: On a key material contract underpinning the deal, Larry J. Ellison and an associated trust entered into a guarantee in favor of WBD, jointly and severally guaranteeing certain PSKY payments under the merger agreement, including $45.72 billion of the merger consideration.
proposals.quote: Ellison and an associated trust entered into a guarantee in favor of WBD to, among other things, jointly and severally guarantee certain payments by PSKY under the PSKY Merger Agreement, including $45.72 billion of the Merger Consideration, and assist WBD with the consummation of the PSKY Merger.
proposals.locator: Item 8, Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements (PSKY Merger Agreement / Ellison guarantee)

proposals.statement: After the DOJ cleared the merger (June 12, 2026) with no divestitures, the principal remaining litigation/close risk is a threatened multi-state action: state attorneys general may still file a lawsuit challenging the merger on antitrust grounds, and the companies face a potential lawsuit by a coalition of state attorneys general who say the Trump administration is failing to enforce antitrust law.

source.title: CNN Business — Trump's DOJ approves Paramount-Warner Bros. merger, as potential state lawsuits loom (June 12, 2026)

proposals.quote: State attorneys general may still file a lawsuit challenging the merger on antitrust grounds.

proposals.statement: California is the named state actively reviewing the deal: a spokesperson for California Attorney General Rob Bonta said, in response to the DOJ decision, that the merger of Warner Bros and Paramount remains under investigation by the California Department of Justice.

source.title: CNN Business — Trump's DOJ approves Paramount-Warner Bros. merger, as potential state lawsuits loom (June 12, 2026)

proposals.quote: A spokesperson for California attorney general Rob Bonta said in response to the DOJ decision that “the merger of Warner Bros and Paramount remains under investigation by the California Department of Justice.”
proposals.locator: CNN Business, June 12, 2026 (Bonta / California DOJ section)

proposals.statement: The litigation profile is process- and deal-driven rather than operational: the live suits (PSKY's own Chancery complaint, the 14D-9 fiduciary-duty suit, the Nicosia merger-objection suit plus 15 demand letters) are disclosure/process claims typical of contested public-company deals — generally settled via supplemental disclosure rather than capable of blocking the deal — whereas the genuine close risk is the threatened multi-state attorneys-general antitrust action, which, coming after a no-conditions DOJ clearance, is the one piece of litigation that could seek to enjoin closing.
